title: United States: End attacks on anti-ICE protesters and civic freedoms activists
content:
As federal agents raid anti-ICE protesters and a voting rights group and the administration targets critics, CIVICUS warns of an increasing assault on US civic space. 

Johannesburg, 19 June 2026 — CIVICUS, the global civil society alliance, is alarmed by the growing number and rapid pace of assaults by the United States government targeting civil society and activists. 

This week, federal agents arrested 12 people from Direct Action Minnesota who protested against Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents. They and three others have been accused of conspiring to impede the Trump administration's Operation Metro Surge targeting Minnesota’s Latino, Somali and Southeast Asian communities. The largest operation in the history of the Department of Homeland Security has reported 4,000 arrests by federal agents, often wearing masks and travelling in unmarked SUVs. 

Some of the protesters were conditionally released by courts without bail. They face conspiracy charges of impeding or injuring federal agents, which could result in imprisonment of up to six years. Attacks against protesters and inflated charges are a gross violation of civic freedoms.
snippet: As federal agents raid anti-ICE protesters and a voting rights group and the administration targets critics, CIVICUS warns of an increasing assault on US civic space.

title: A Public Call to end Khurram Parvez’ arbitrary detention
content:
On his 49th birthday, his fifth while arbitrarily detained, we, the undersigned organisations and individuals, stand in solidarity with human rights defender Khurram Parvez. We demand that the Indian authorities drop all charges against him, release him immediately and unconditionally, and cease their campaign of judicial harassment.

We have followed with concern Khurram Parvez’s detention on baseless charges since November 2021, and his continued detention without trial in Delhi’s Rohini Jail. Tomorrow, on his birthday, he will have spent 1,670 days in prison.

We remind the Indian authorities that three years ago, in June 2023, the UN Working Group on Arbitrary Detention (UNWGAD) determined that Khurram Parvez’s detention was arbitrary and called on the Indian authorities to release him. We are concerned that the Indian government, despite pledging to participate meaningfully with UN mechanisms as a member of the UN Human Rights Council, has not engaged with the UNWGAD in relation to Khurram’s detention.

title: Indonesia: Deliver justice for attack on activist by armed forces
content:
CIVICUS condemns the inadequate and unjust sentences by the military for the acid attack on human rights defender Andrie Yunus. The global civil society alliance calls for the case to be taken up in the civilian justice system.  Instead of serving as an effective deterrent, it will embolden those targeting activists in Indonesia. Those involved in the attack – including the masterminds – must be brought to justice.

On 12 March 2026, human rights defender Andrie Yunus was subjected to an acid attack in Central Jakarta, immediately after recording a podcast on the militarisation of civil and political affairs in Indonesia. The attack has caused severe and permanent injuries requiring long-term treatment, including acid burns affecting the eyes, skin, and joints.

Four members of the Indonesian National Armed Forces were detained, subsequently charged and have been sentenced between 1.5 to 3 years in prison.
